unresolved: Semantic ambiguity of the issue: the changing method is the 2-arg `deserialize(JsonParser, DeserializationContext)`. The sibling 3-arg overload `deserialize(JsonParser, DeserializationContext, T intoValue)` is NOT assumed to change its own signature; its overrides are included ONLY where their body calls the 2-arg variant (call sites). If the intent is to change ALL `deserialize` overloads, the 3-arg overrides (e.g. StdScalarDeserializer/PrimitiveArrayDeserializers/ReferenceTypeDeserializer/CollectionDeserializer/MapDeserializer/UntypedObjectDeserializer 3-arg variants and JsonDeserializer.java 3-arg default) would additionally need signature changes.

Test-side enumeration risk: src/test contains many small/anonymous custom deserializers. The list was gathered systematically (grep + per-file reads) but anonymous-class overrides are easy to miss and several are labeled by line (deserialize@LINE) rather than a stable symbol name; a couple of these may need on-edit confirmation. test-jdk11/test-jdk21 roots showed no matches but were only grep-scanned.

A few private helper method names at confirmed call-site lines (e.g. MapDeserializer._readAndBind*/_readAndUpdate*, StringCollectionDeserializer._deserializeFromArray/_deserializeNotArray) were resolved via sub-agent reads; the file+receiver are verified but the exact enclosing-method name should be confirmed against the line when editing.

Dynamic dispatch: call sites were resolved by static receiver type. Calls through `JsonDeserializer<?>`-typed fields/variables are included; if a fix also touches reflective/serialized deserializer usage that cannot be found by source search, those are not captured.
